name: Brownie Cakes in a Jar
recipeInstructions:
1. Sterilize two 1-pint straight-sided wide-mouth canning jars (specifically made for canning jams and jellies) by boiling them in water for 10 minutes. Keep the lids and rings in the hot water until ready to use; set aside.
2. In a small bowl, stir together flour, sugar, baking soda, and cinnamon (if desired).
3. In a medium saucepan, combine butter or margarine, water, and cocoa powder. Heat and stir until butter is melted and mixture is well blended. Remove from heat and stir in the dry ingredients.
4. Add buttermilk, egg, and vanilla; beat by hand until smooth. Stir in walnut pieces.
5. Pour mixture into prepared canning jars. Place jars onto a cookie sheet. Preheat oven to 325°F.
6. Bake for 35-40 minutes or until a pick inserted deep into each cake comes out clean. Remove cakes from the oven, one at a time.
7. Place a lid on each jar, then place a ring on top and screw down tightly. Use heavy-duty mitts; the jars are hot. Let cool completely on a counter. You'll hear a "plinking" sound when they seal.
8. If lids don't move when pressed, the cakes are sealed. Store in a cool, dark place for up to a year. For decoration, place cotton in the center of each lid, then decorate with cloth (cut 3-inches larger than the lid), ribbon, or dried flowers. Secure the ring. Use your imagination and hot glue gun for best results. These make wonderful gifts.
